mnemonics: SCCR - Set, Get, Check, Remember: The cookie lifecycle - Server creates, Client stores, Server checks on next request, Client remembers state.
keyPoints: Cookies are small pieces of data stored on the client side by the web browser.
They are used to maintain state and track information about the user across multiple requests.
In Java, the javax.servlet.http.Cookie class is used to create, set, and retrieve cookies.
commonMistakes: Storing sensitive information (like passwords) in cookies, as they are visible and can be manipulated on the client side.
Not setting an appropriate expiration time, leading to cookies that persist longer than needed or are session-only when persistence is required.
